proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response in AML patients.

analyses.result_summary: Univariable logistic regression of objective_response on ecog_ps: log OR = -0.384 per 1-point increase, OR = 0.681, p ~ 1.5e-97. Strong, highly significant negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with idh1_mutation have a higher probability of objective_response than those without idh1_mutation (overall main effect).

analyses.result_summary: Univariable logistic regression: log OR = +0.364, OR = 1.44, p = 4.1e-18. RR = 22.1% in IDH1-mutated (n=3624) vs 16.5% in IDH1-wildtype (n=46376), absolute difference +5.6 pp.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with a higher probability of objective_response than not receiving it (marginal main effect).

analyses.result_summary: Marginal log OR = +0.053, OR = 1.05, p = 0.031. RR = 17.4% on ven-aza vs 16.6% off (absolute +0.7 pp). Statistically significant but small in magnitude — suggests benefit may be concentrated in specific subgroups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between idh1_mutation and treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine: IDH1-mutated patients gain more benefit (higher objective_response rate) from venetoclax-azacitidine than IDH1-wildtype patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term log OR = +0.730, p = 7.7e-18. 4-way response rates: IDH1-/ven-aza- = 16.6%; IDH1-/ven-aza+ = 16.4%; IDH1+/ven-aza- = 17.1%; IDH1+/ven-aza+ = 29.8%. Adding ven-aza adds ~13 pp absolute response in IDH1-mutated patients but essentially nothing in IDH1-wildtype. Interaction persists after multivariable adjustment (logOR=+0.765, p=4.4e-19).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between idh1_mutation and treatment_ivosidenib: IDH1-mutated patients respond better to ivosidenib than IDH1-wildtype patients (consistent with IDH1's known role as ivosidenib's target).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term log OR = -0.364, p = 0.027 — direction OPPOSITE to the matched-pair expectation. Among IDH1-mutated, RR = 17.5% on ivosidenib vs 22.6% off (-5 pp); among IDH1-wildtype, RR = 17.1% on ivosidenib vs 16.5% off (+0.6 pp). Refutes the hypothesis: in this dataset ivosidenib does NOT preferentially benefit IDH1-mutated patients and may even reduce their response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between idh2_mutation and treatment_enasidenib: IDH2-mutated patients respond better to enasidenib than IDH2-wildtype patients (matched-pair targeted-therapy hypothesis).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log OR = -0.124, p = 0.45 — not significant. Among IDH2-mutated, RR = 14.4% on enasidenib vs 16.7% off; among IDH2-wildtype, RR = 16.3% on enasidenib vs 17.0% off. No evidence of a matched-pair benefit for enasidenib in IDH2-mutated patients in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between npm1_mutation and treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine: NPM1-mutated patients gain more benefit from venetoclax-azacitidine than NPM1-wildtype patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log OR = +0.261, p = 6.7e-7. NPM1-mutated patients gain a meaningful boost on ven-aza relative to NPM1-wildtype, supporting the hypothesis. Persists in multivariable model (logOR=+0.270, p=3.6e-7).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a negative interaction between npm1_mutation and treatment_ivosidenib: NPM1-mutated patients respond worse to ivosidenib than NPM1-wildtype patients (i.e., NPM1+ patients should not be put on a non-IDH targeted therapy when alternatives exist).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log OR = -0.225, p = 0.024. Confirms a negative interaction — NPM1-mutated patients see worse response on ivosidenib than NPM1-wildtype patients, consistent with the hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a negative interaction between complex_karyotype and treatment_7plus3: complex-karyotype patients fare worse on 7+3 induction than non-complex-karyotype patients (the 'high-risk on intensive chemotherapy' hypothesis).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log OR = -0.213, p = 0.0016. 7+3 is associated with a meaningful response reduction in complex-karyotype patients relative to its effect in non-complex-karyotype patients, supporting the hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: tp53_mutation is associated with a lower probability of objective_response (main effect; classic high-risk AML mutation).
Among tp53_mutation+ patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine yields a higher objective_response rate than 7+3 (subgroup test).

analyses.result_summary: Univariable log OR = -0.027, p = 0.51. RR = 16.6% in TP53+ vs 16.9% in TP53-. No detectable main effect of TP53 mutation on response in this cohort — refutes the classic prognostic association at the response endpoint.
Within TP53+ subgroup (n=4985): RR on ven-aza = 16.4% vs off 16.7%; RR on 7+3 = 16.4% vs off 16.7%. No subgroup-specific treatment effect detected. Refutes h10b.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between flt3_itd and treatment_midostaurin (or treatment_gilteritinib): FLT3-ITD-positive patients respond preferentially to FLT3 inhibitors.

analyses.result_summary: FLT3-ITD × midostaurin interaction log OR = -0.105, p = 0.22. FLT3-ITD × gilteritinib interaction log OR = +0.034, p = 0.69. Neither interaction is significant. Among FLT3-ITD+ patients, RR on midostaurin = 16.1% vs off 17.4%; RR on gilteritinib = 16.9% vs off 17.2%. Hypothesis is REFUTED — no evidence of FLT3-inhibitor benefit specific to FLT3-ITD+ patients in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with a higher probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Log OR per +1 g/dL albumin = +0.115, OR = 1.12, p = 1.4e-6. Significant positive association consistent with albumin as a marker of nutrition / inflammation / hepatic function. Persists after multivariable adjustment (logOR=+0.118, p=1e-6).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Greater 6-month weight loss (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Log OR per 1% weight loss = -0.040, OR = 0.96, p = 7.4e-35. Strong negative association. Each additional 5% of body weight lost is associated with ~OR 0.82 reduction in odds of response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher baseline marrow blast percentage (blast_pct_marrow) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Log OR per 1% increase in blast burden = -0.0058, OR = 0.994, p = 1.2e-19. Each 10-percentage-point increase in marrow blasts is associated with ~OR 0.94 in response. Significant inverse association consistent with disease burden effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) and higher peripheral WBC (wbc_k_per_ul) are each associated with a lower probability of objective_response (inflammation / disease-burden hypothesis).

analyses.result_summary: CRP: log OR = -0.0070 per mg/L, p = 2.8e-6 (significant). WBC: log OR = -0.0022 per k/uL, p = 1.9e-5 (significant). Both are negatively associated with response, supporting the hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between unfit_for_intensive and treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine: ven-aza preferentially benefits patients deemed unfit for intensive chemotherapy.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log OR = +0.104, p = 0.036. Among unfit patients, RR on ven-aza = 17.9% vs off 16.3% (+1.6 pp); among fit, RR on ven-aza = 17.0% vs off 16.8% (+0.2 pp). Modest but significant interaction supports the hypothesis. Within the unfit subgroup, 7+3 shows no benefit (16.9% on vs 17.0% off).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients identifying as race_ethnicity == 'black' have a lower probability of objective_response than other patients (disparity hypothesis).
Patients identifying as race_ethnicity == 'white' have a higher probability of objective_response than other patients.

analyses.result_summary: Log OR for black vs all other = -0.096, p = 0.011. Black RR = 15.7% vs 17.1% in non-black. Significant unadjusted disparity in objective response.
Log OR for white vs all other = +0.078, p = 0.002. White RR = 17.3% vs 16.0% in non-white. Significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with insurance_type == 'medicaid' or 'uninsured' have a lower probability of objective_response than those with private or medicare coverage (socioeconomic disparity hypothesis).
Rural residence (rural_residence) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: medicaid: log OR = -0.003, p = 0.94; uninsured: log OR = -0.021, p = 0.72; medicare: log OR = +0.034, p = 0.16; private: log OR = -0.030, p = 0.22. None significant — REFUTES the insurance-disparity hypothesis at the response endpoint.
Log OR = +0.0001, p = 0.99 — null, refutes the rural-disparity hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: snp_rs1050828 carrier status is associated with a higher probability of objective_response (pharmacogenomic main effect).

analyses.result_summary: Log OR = +0.118, p = 0.0053. RR = 18.4% in carriers vs 16.7% in non-carriers (n_carrier=4025). Mildly significant positive association. With 24 SNPs tested, this might survive a Bonferroni-style correction (alpha=0.0021) only borderline; treat as a candidate signal worth replication.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The remaining 23 SNPs (excluding rs1050828) have no individual main-effect association with objective_response (null hypothesis at standard alpha=0.05 after multiple-testing context).

analyses.code: for s in [c for c in df.columns if c.startswith('snp_') and c!='snp_rs1050828']: sm.Logit(df['objective_response'], sm.add_constant(df[[s]].astype(float))).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Of 23 remaining SNPs, only snp_rs2032582 reached nominal significance (logOR=-0.048, p=0.049). All others have p > 0.05 (next: rs1799853 p=0.054, rs3813867 p=0.061, rs1801133 p=0.16). Consistent with chance/noise — supports the hypothesis that the SNP panel as a whole is largely uninformative for response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age (age_years) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response in this AML cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Log OR per year = +0.0004, p = 0.72. Null. After adjustment for ECOG, weight loss, blast %, albumin, age remains null. REFUTES the age-prognosis hypothesis at the response endpoint — the apparent age effect in raw clinical reasoning is captured entirely by performance status and disease-burden markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Solid-tumor / non-AML markers in this dataset (her2_amplification, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, braf_v600e, ntrk_fusion, msi_high, liver_mets, bone_mets, pleural_effusion, ca_125, cea, psa, pik3ca_mutation, pten_loss, fgfr_alteration, cdkn2a_loss, keap1_mutation) have no association with objective_response in an AML cohort.

analyses.result_summary: All 18 solid-tumor / non-AML markers tested have p > 0.05 (smallest p = 0.10 for psa_ng_ml; her2 p=0.83, msi_high p=0.14, all others p>0.20). Hypothesis is SUPPORTED — these features are essentially uninformative for AML objective_response, consistent with their irrelevance to AML biology.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Atrial fibrillation is associated with a lower probability of objective_response (cardiotoxicity / frailty interaction with chemotherapy).

Among the broader comorbidity panel (diabetes, hypertension, COPD, CKD, heart failure, CAD, prior malignancy, autoimmune disease, hepatitis B/C, HIV), most have no main-effect association with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Log OR = -0.101, p = 0.024. RR = 15.6% with afib (n=4143) vs 17.0% without. Significant modest negative association supporting the hypothesis.

Of the 11 listed comorbidities, none reach p<0.05 (smallest p=0.10 for hiv_positive logOR=-0.21; CKD p=0.13, copd p=0.14, all others p>0.29). Supports the hypothesis — most non-cardiac comorbidities are not main-effect predictors of response in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher appetite_loss_grade is associated with a lower probability of objective_response, while other patient-reported symptom grades (fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade) are not (selectivity hypothesis).

analyses.result_summary: appetite_loss_grade: log OR = -0.023 per grade, p = 0.038 (significant). Other symptom grades: fatigue p=0.21, pain_nrs p=0.34, dyspnea p=0.07, cough p=0.35 — none significant at 0.05. Supports the selectivity hypothesis; appetite loss appears to track disease burden / cachexia in a way other symptoms do not.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic-regression model adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, fitness, comorbidity proxies, key AML mutations, baseline labs, and treatments, the strongest independent predictors of objective_response are: (a) ECOG performance status (negative), (b) idh1_mutation × treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine interaction (positive, large), (c) blast_pct_marrow (negative), (d) weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), (e) albumin_g_dl (positive), and (f) npm1_mutation × treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine interaction (positive). Older age, sex, secondary AML, TP53 mutation, complex karyotype main effect, FLT3-ITD/TKD main effect, and most treatments' main effects are NOT independent predictors after these.

analyses.code: Multivariable logistic regression with main effects (age,sex,ECOG,unfit,secondary_aml,complex_karyotype,FLT3-ITD/TKD,IDH1,IDH2,NPM1,TP53,WBC,blast%,albumin,CRP,weight loss,6 treatments) plus 6 interaction terms (idh1_x_venaza, idh1_x_ivo, npm1_x_venaza, npm1_x_ivo, cmplx_x_7p3, unfit_x_venaza).
analyses.result_summary: Top predictors by p-value: ecog_ps (logOR=-0.386, p~1.5e-97); weight_loss_pct_6mo (-0.041, p=6.8e-35); blast_pct_marrow (-0.0058, p=2.4e-20); idh1_x_venaza (+0.766, p=4.4e-19); npm1_x_venaza (+0.270, p=3.6e-7); albumin_g_dl (+0.118, p=1.0e-6); crp_mg_l (-0.007, p=1.4e-6); wbc_k_per_ul (-0.0022, p=1.2e-5); cmplx_x_7p3 (-0.217, p=0.0015); npm1_x_ivo (-0.221, p=0.028); unfit_x_venaza (+0.106, p=0.034); idh1_x_ivo (-0.349, p=0.037). Non-significant after adjustment: age (p=0.79), sex (p=0.29), TP53 (p=0.36), secondary_aml (p=0.93), unfit_for_intensive main (p=0.25), idh1_mutation main (p=0.43), npm1_mutation main (p=0.13), FLT3-ITD/TKD (p=0.52, 0.44), idh2_mutation (p=0.89), midostaurin (p=0.97), gilteritinib (p=0.11), enasidenib (p=0.19). Hypothesis SUPPORTED.
